opts: Shoot really hard from super far out
Skate closer to the net and then shoot
Pass to a teammate farther from the net
Stand still and wait for somebody to help
Refuse to try the new drill
Try your best even if it's
Watch other kids do it first
Tell coach you want to stop
Nothing — you got there first, that's good
The referee calls offsides and play stops
You score a goal for being fast to arrive
The other team gets to take a free shot
Skate over and argue with the referee
Say mean things to the other team's bench
Skate back to your position and get ready
Sit down on the ice and take a small break
The game just keeps on going and no whistle even blows
Play stops and there's a faceoff to restart the game
The team that shot it gets a two minute penalty
Nothing — the ref just tosses in a new puck fast
Stay quiet so you don't ruin their play
'Man on!' or 'Puck!' so they know it's coming
'Watch out!' quietly so they barely hear you
Wait to see what happens on your own out there
Do your own thing for the whole game and tell the coach about it later
Argue with the coach right now in front of the whole team out there
Try coach's way one shift, then skip the rest of the game
Refuse to play the coach's way and sit on the bench a while
Your goalie made a really huge mistake on the last goal and coach wants a very different player on the ice
Your goalie is tired and wants a long rest on the bench, so coach is swapping in a skater instead
Having an extra skater gives your team a better chance to score and tie the game — even though your ne
Your goalie needs a quick rest and a drink of water, so coach is putting another skater on the ice
Feel really embarrassed in front of the whole team and want to give up on hockey for good
Argue that the coach is wrong about the video and tell everybody it was somebody else's fault
Listen carefully — coaches show video to help the whole team learn, not to embarrass anyone
Pretend you weren't watching the video and look down at your skates until the meeting ends
Your team is skating way too slow to ever get to the puck, so the other team keeps winning every race to it
Everyone is in the same spot — some players should be spread out, especially someone covering the front of the net
Your goalie should skate out of the net and help grab the puck from the corner so the whole team can get going again
The coach should call a quick timeout and tell every player to calm down and skate back to their own spots
They should have shot the puck at the other team's net from way back at center ice before they lost the puck in the first place
They're standing still instead of skating back — when the other team has the puck heading to your net, you skate back to help defend
They should skate to the bench and let a fresh new teammate jump on the ice and chase the puck to help stop the other team from scoring
They need brand new skates because their old skates are too slow to keep up with the fastest skaters on the other team this season
The ice was super bumpy and the puck bounced funny off the stick when you took the shot from center ice today
You shot from too far away — skate closer to the net first so your shot is harder and more accurate
Your stick is way too small and you need a bigger one to shoot much harder from the far end
The goalie on the other team is really good and can stop pretty much every shot coming at the net
They need to skate way a lot faster with the puck so the other team can't catch up and knock it off their stick out there
They're not passing to open teammates — hockey is a team game and passing to open players creates better scoring chances
They should switch positions with another teammate on the line so they don't have to carry the puck as much each shift
Their stick is the wrong size and it keeps making them lose the puck every time they try to turn or skate hard with it
They should have been way faster off the starting line for the race and gotten a better jump on the other player out there
They gave up — even if you lose the race, keep skating. You can still pressure the player or get open for the next play.
They should skate to the bench and let a fresh teammate jump on the ice to take over and chase down the puck for them
The other player cheated by pushing off with a hand at the start, and the ref should have called a penalty on that play
The ref was wrong about the call — nothing bad even happened on that play so nobody should get a penalty there at all
They kept playing after the whistle — when the whistle blows, you stop. Pushing someone after the whistle is a penalty.
The opponent started the push first before the whistle blew and the referee must have missed the start of the play there
They were just finishing a check they started right before the whistle, and that sort of thing is supposed to be allowed
The drill sounds really boring and shooting pucks is a lot more fun, so the coach should pick a different drill the kids like doing better
They're not listening to the coach — when the coach gives instructions, everyone stops and listens. That's how the whole team gets better together.
They need way more practice time shooting pucks so they can get better at hitting the net, and coach should let them shoot longer before every team drill
The coach should let them keep shooting because shooting practice is more helpful than standing in a line, that's how young kids get better at hockey
They're in a really good hiding spot to surprise the other team later in the play when the puck comes back around
They're hiding behind the goalie instead of being in a useful position — get to open ice where you can help your team
They should talk to the goalie about what's happening up the ice so the goalie is ready for the next big shot to come in
They need a small rest during the play and behind the net is the safest spot to take one out of everybody's way
You skate away from the loose puck and go back toward your own end
You wait for another one of your own teammates to come skate over and grab it
You shoot the puck at the net right there and the goalie is out of position
You pass it back to your teammate who took the shot a brief moment ago
You keep playing and try to knock the puck out of the goalie's glove before the ref stops the play today out there
Everyone stops and there's a faceoff in your zone — use this time to catch your breath and get to your position
You skate over to the bench and sit down to get a drink of water before the game starts up again there
The game is over right then and both teams line up at center ice and shake hands with each other fast
You stop skating and wait for your teammate to get back up before you do anything else
You pick up the loose puck and keep the play going — your teammate will get up and join back in
You fall down on the ice too so your teammate is not the only one down over there by themselves
You stop skating and go over to ask if they are okay before you keep playing any hockey
The whole game ends right there because the other team got a really bad penalty against them today
Your team gets a power play — you have more players on the ice than them, so this is a great chance to score
Nothing really changes — the game just keeps going along exactly the same way it was going earlier on in the period
Both teams each lose a player from the ice until all the penalty time is totally done being served
You both stop and wait to see who decides to go first for the puck
Whoever skates harder gets there first — race to the puck and compete for it
You just let the other player have the puck so nobody crashes into the boards
You skate over to the bench and change lines with one of your teammates
You keep celebrating out on the ice for the whole rest of the game with all of your teammates there
You celebrate quickly with your teammates, then skate back to center ice and line up for the next faceoff
You skate straight over to the bench to tell the coach all about the goal you just scored today
You do a really long dance in front of the net before finally skating back to center ice to get ready again
You keep sitting on the bench and wait for coach to say your name out loud before you get up
You stand up, get to the door, and get ready to jump on the ice as soon as your teammates come off
You start stretching out your legs on the bench so you are warm and ready to go on the ice
You ask the coach which line you are on so you know when it is time to go out on the ice
